title: Energy Use Intensity (EUI) Definition
summary: EUI is total energy consumed by the building in one year divided by total gross floor area.

structured_payload.formula: total energy consumed / total gross floor area

evidence_quote:
EUI: Energy Use Intensity
total energy consumed by the building in one year (measured in kBtu or GJ) / total gross floor area of the building (measured in square feet or square meters)

title: Energy Cost Index (ECI) Definition
summary: ECI is total cost of energy consumed by the building in one year divided by total gross floor area.

structured_payload.formula: total cost of energy consumed / total gross floor area

evidence_quote:
ECI: Energy Cost Index
total cost energy consumed by the building in one year (measured in $) / total gross floor area of the building (measured in square feet or square meters)

title: Preliminary Energy Use Analysis Data Requirements
summary: Preliminary energy use analysis requires utility bills and schedules for at least one calendar year.

structured_payload.requirement: minimum 1 calendar year of utility bills and schedules

evidence_quote: Data Sources: utility bills, schedules (min. 1 calendar year)

title: Model Calibration Tolerance
summary: Energy model calibration should be within ±10% of real energy consumption.
evidence_quote: Good practice: it should be in the range of ±10%

title: Audit Team Composition for Level 1
summary: Basic team for Level 1 audit includes energy auditor (solo) and optionally an energy analyst.
evidence_quote:
Basic Team –Level 1:
The energy auditor (solo), level 1 and probably level 2 depending on the facility
Opt: Energy Analyst

title: Audit Team Composition for Level 2
summary: For Level 2 audit, consider adding surveyor/drafter, energy modeler, and optionally discipline specialists.
evidence_quote:
For a level 2, consider adding:
Surveyor / Drafter (if there are no drawings)
Energy modeler (if the auditor is not the energy modeler)
Opt: Discipline specialist:
Mechanical engineer
Electrical Engineer
Power Engineer

title: Audit Team Composition for Level 3
summary: For Level 3 audit, consider adding a second auditor, electrician, and optional specialists.
evidence_quote:
For a Level 3, consider adding:
Second auditor, specialist in one specific aspect of the audit (i.e. LEED professional)
Electrician on site: to install data loggers and energy analyzers
Opt: Light specialist
Opt: Emissions Specialist
Any additional technician

title: Site Visit Activities Sequence
summary: Site visits include preliminary walkthrough, safety toolbox meeting, equipment checks, data collection, interviews, and documentation.
evidence_quote:
Preliminary Walkthrough
SAFETY toolbox meeting
Equipment Checks
Data Collection
Interviews
Documentation

title: Energy Audit Report Structure
summary: Report includes executive summary, background, existing systems, EEMs, and supporting information.

sections: Description of the Existing Building Systems

evidence_quote:
1. Executive Summary
2. Background Information
3. Description of the Existing Building Systems
4. Energy Efficiency Measures
5. Supporting Information

title: Energy Efficiency Measures Categories
summary: EEMs include no-cost/low-cost, capital investment, demand response, distributed generation/renewable, and O&M measures.
evidence_quote:
No-cost/low-cost measures
Capital investment measures
Demand response measures
Distributed generation/renewable energy measures
O&M measures
